system_description.overview: AltiSales is an AI-powered Analytics, Coaching, and Optimization platform called Caddy designed to help companies understand the root cause of their pipeline generation inefficiencies and deploy solutions that maximize performance. The platform leverages integration partners to gather anonymized data, uses Big Data analysis and statistical inference for continuous monitoring, and surfaces relevant insights to the right person at the right time.

products.Automated GTM Data Analysis: Integration and monitoring capabilities within client environments, alerts and task management in a Command Center, overview of static/timeline/trending data with multiple filters, deep insights into optimizing infrastructure like reducing bounce rates and optimizing phone numbers.
products.Coaching & Troubleshooting Capabilities: Email and Phone Coaching Workflows for frontline managers, Slack and Teams integrations for closed loop feedback, flagging emerging problems to infrastructure owners via email or messaging platforms, visibility into lead bottlenecks or Account penetration, command center with task management and reminders.
products.Support Services & Optimization Features: Comprehensive white-glove onboarding and implementation support, Power Dialer, CallerID technology, email validation solutions, access to dedicated GTM expertise and enablement documentation.

infrastructure.cloud_provider: AWS (carve-out - AWS controls excluded from scope, AltiSales responsibilities for applications and services on AWS are in scope)
infrastructure.physical_presence: No physical offices or servers; all data processed on cloud services. HQ address listed as 2900 NE 7th Ave, unit 4401, Miami, FL 33137
infrastructure.availability_zones: Multi-AZ (production servers hosted in high availability data centers with multiple availability zones)

network_architecture.network_segmentation: Segmented VPCs with access control lists (ACLs). Network segmentation implemented to separate systems and data based on sensitivity and criticality.
network_architecture.api_gateway: Amazon API Gateway (visible in network diagram)
network_architecture.waf: AWS WAF (CloudFront WAF/Shield visible in network diagram, described as application-level SOC 2, L4 DDoS via Compute Shield)
network_architecture.firewalls: System firewalls configured on application gateway and production network to limit unnecessary ports, protocols and services. Firewall rules reviewed annually by IT management.
network_architecture.ids_ips: IDS and IPS via AWS to identify and protect against threats in conjunction with security policy network settings
network_architecture.vpn: Remote access done through a bastion host with a virtual private network (VPN)
network_architecture.tls: TLS using certificates from established third-party certificate authority for all web-based system requests
network_architecture.content_filtering: All systems restricted to internet with content filtering system routed through proxy server
network_architecture.topology: End User -> HTTPS/TLS -> CloudFront (WAF/Shield, Application-level SOC 2, L4 DDoS via Compute Shield) -> HTTPS/TLS (Auth) -> Amazon API Gateway -> VPC/SQL IAM -> AWS Lambda (in VPC) -> Inter-VPC Encrypted in Transit -> Amazon RDS (MySQL, Encrypted at Rest, Multi-AZ, No public access) -> AWS Secrets Manager. Also: RDS -> Encrypted in Transit, Secrets -> Amazon CloudWatch (Logging, Monitoring). External APIs: Outreach API, Guesscx API connected via HTTPS/TLS with OAuth2 and Token/Rate Limiting.

application_architecture.api_style: REST (API Gateway pattern visible in diagram)
application_architecture.environments: Separate development/test and production environments. Changes tested in separate environment before implementation (CC8.1.4).

authentication_access_control.mfa: MFA enforced for user accounts with administrative access to production platform (CC6.1.1). MFA enforced for all remote access (CC6.1.1 test). MFA required for source code tool access (CC8.1.3). Two-factor authentication required for employee email access.
authentication_access_control.access_revocation_sla: Within 1 business day upon termination of employment
authentication_access_control.password_policy: Controlled through AWS Password policy with periodic forced changes, password expiry and complexity requirements. Accounts disabled after limited number of unsuccessful logon attempts.

encryption.in_transit: TLS (version not specified, using certificates from established third-party CA)
encryption.at_rest: Cloud provider key management service (AWS KMS implied via CC6.1.5 - uses cloud provider KMS to encrypt data at rest and manage encryption keys)
encryption.key_management: AWS KMS (cloud provider key management service). Access to encryption keys restricted to authorized personnel only (CC6.1.9).

ci_cd_devops.source_control: Source code management tool (not named; source code changes are logged, time-stamped, and attributed to author - CC8.1.3)
ci_cd_devops.branch_protection: Repository branch rules configured to ensure every merge request to move change to production environment requires additional approval (CC8.1.2). Developers do not make changes to application code in production without additional approval.
ci_cd_devops.iac: Configuration management tool used for implementation and rollback capability (IaC implied via CC7.1.1 - baseline configurations retained within infrastructure as code)
ci_cd_devops.patch_management: CTO/Management has set up automatic patch management and performs monthly patch management reviews. Workstations configured for automatic updates and scanned for patch compliance daily.

monitoring_logging.log_aggregation: Amazon CloudWatch (Logging, Monitoring - visible in network diagram)
monitoring_logging.alerting: Automated alerts configured to notify IT management of high and critical risk events. Monitoring tool generates alerts when specific predefined thresholds are met.

third_party_services.purpose: Primary cloud infrastructure / subservice organization for data center services
Internal communication and closed-loop feedback integrations
Internal communication and closed-loop feedback integrations

security_tools.antivirus: Anti-virus and Malware protection software installed on all production servers (continuous scanning) and Mac-based workstations/laptops. Definitions updated weekly for production servers.
security_tools.vulnerability_scanning: Monthly automated vulnerability scans on infrastructure and applications, with scan frequency adjusted as required. Performed by internal IT Infrastructure team. Self-assessment scan of vulnerabilities performed prior to every release or yearly.
security_tools.penetration_testing: Annual external penetration testing conducted by independent third-party. Results reviewed by IT management. High/critical findings remediated in timely manner and added to risk register.

untestable: CC2.2.3 - Could not be tested because there were no significant changes to people, roles, and responsibilities during the engagement period
CC3.2.1 - Cybersecurity insurance could not be tested because there were no cyber incidents reported during the engagement period
CC6.5.2 - Customer data deletion upon termination could not be tested because there were no customer terminations during the engagement period
CC7.3.1 - Incident response could not be tested because there were no security incidents reported during the engagement period
excluded_criteria: CC6.4 - Physical access to facilities: AWS is responsible for physical security; no physical security controls in scope
A1.2 - Environmental protections: AltiSales does not have an office/operates from working space managed by building management; all environmental controls managed by building management/AWS
Processing Integrity - Determined not relevant to the system
Privacy - Determined not relevant to the system
notable_controls: CC8.1.2 - Branch protection with merge request approval required for production deployments
CC8.1.3 - Source code changes logged, time-stamped, attributed to author; source code tool access restricted via MFA
CC7.1.1 - Infrastructure as code with baseline configurations and configuration management tool for rollback
CC6.1.7 - Network segmentation to separate systems by sensitivity and criticality
CC7.4.1 - BCDR plan documented, reviewed annually, tested annually with different loss scenarios
CC7.4.2 - Daily backups with multi-AZ configuration and alerting for backup failures
CC4.1.1 - Annual external penetration testing by independent third-party
CC2.1.2 - Risk and Governance Executive Committee (RGEC) meeting semiannually to review internal controls
CC3.1.1 - IT Leadership Committee meeting weekly to review security activities and processes

infrastructure.rationale: AWS-hosted with VPC, multi-AZ, API Gateway, WAF, and CloudFront CDN. However, single-region only (no multi-region mentioned), no specific regions disclosed, and limited detail on infrastructure diversity. IaC mentioned but tool not named.

application_architecture.rationale: Serverless Lambda-based architecture with API Gateway is a modern pattern. However, no programming languages, frameworks, or specific tooling disclosed. No mention of testing practices, CI/CD pipeline tooling, or containerization. Separate environments confirmed.

data_layer.rationale: RDS MySQL with multi-AZ and encryption at rest. Daily backups confirmed. Data classification and retention policies in place. Per-tenant segregation mentioned (CC6.1.3). However, single database type, no caching layer, no data warehouse, no file storage mentioned.

security_posture.rationale: Strong security posture: WAF + IDS/IPS + annual pen testing + monthly vulnerability scanning + endpoint protection + MFA everywhere + network segmentation + firewalls + cybersecurity insurance. Content filtering via proxy. Log protection in place. Access reviews quarterly. Incident response plan documented. Missing: no SIEM tool named, no specific endpoint protection vendor named.

devops_maturity.rationale: Branch protection with merge request approval, IaC for baseline configurations, separate environments, emergency change process documented. However, no CI/CD tool named, no source control platform named, no container orchestration, no specific deployment pipeline described. Monthly patch management reviews.

bcdr_readiness.rationale: BCDR policy documented and reviewed annually. Annual testing with different loss scenarios. Daily backups with multi-AZ. Multi-AZ failover for production servers. However, no RTO/RPO disclosed, no multi-region failover, and DR plan in Section 5 was not audited.

vendor_diversity.rationale: Heavily dependent on AWS as sole cloud provider (critical single-vendor dependency). Integration partners (Outreach, Salesloft, Gong) are customer-facing data sources rather than infrastructure vendors. Slack/Teams for communication. No named security vendors, monitoring vendors, or CI/CD vendors diversify the stack.

overall.rationale: AltiSales demonstrates a solid baseline security posture for a small/mid-size SaaS company with clean audit results (0 exceptions). The AWS serverless architecture is modern but details are sparse - many specific technologies, tools, and vendors are not named in the report. The report is heavily template-driven with significant control description repetition. Security fundamentals are covered but technology specificity is low, making deep technical assessment difficult.

red_flags: Very short audit period (3 months: May 8 - Aug 8, 2025) - this is the minimum for Type 2 and suggests this may be their first SOC 2 audit
Heavy single-vendor dependency on AWS with no multi-region failover
No RTO/RPO disclosed despite having a BCDR plan
Processing Integrity and Privacy trust criteria excluded - notable for a platform that handles sales engagement data
Multiple controls untestable due to no events during the 3-month period (no incidents, no terminations, no significant org changes) - suggests very small or new organization
No specific security tooling vendors named (no SIEM, no named endpoint protection, no named vulnerability scanner)
yellow_flags: No programming languages, frameworks, or CI/CD tools disclosed in the report
Source code management tool not named (just 'a source code management tool')
No APM or observability tooling named beyond CloudWatch
Single database type (RDS MySQL) with no caching or data warehouse layer
Report is heavily template-driven with significant repetition of control descriptions across criteria
Section 5 (BCDR details) explicitly noted as not audited
Cybersecurity insurance control untestable - no evidence of actual coverage reviewed
No mention of data residency, GDPR compliance, or geographic data handling
green_flags: Clean unqualified opinion with 0 exceptions across all tested controls
Modern serverless architecture (Lambda + API Gateway) reduces infrastructure management burden
MFA enforced across production, source code, remote access, and email
Network diagram provided showing clear traffic flow with encryption in transit throughout
Quarterly user access reviews for all accounts including administrative privileges
Annual external penetration testing by independent third-party
Monthly vulnerability scanning with remediation tracking
Infrastructure as code with rollback capability
Branch protection requiring merge request approval for production deployments
IT Leadership Committee meeting weekly; Risk and Governance Executive Committee meeting semiannually
Daily backups with multi-AZ configuration and alerting
Formal vendor management program with SOC report reviews and quarterly risk assessments for critical vendors without SOC reports
key_observations: The 3-month audit period (minimum for Type 2) combined with the number of untestable controls strongly suggests this is AltiSales' first SOC 2 report - likely obtained to satisfy customer security requirements for their sales engagement platform

The network diagram reveals more technical detail than the text: CloudFront with WAF/Shield, API Gateway with auth, Lambda in VPC, RDS MySQL (multi-AZ, encrypted, no public access), Secrets Manager, and CloudWatch - this is a fairly standard AWS serverless stack
External API connections to Outreach API and 'Guesscx API' (likely internal naming) use OAuth2 with token and rate limiting, showing awareness of API security
The report mentions both a CEO and CTO by role, plus a COO and VP of Revenue Operations - relatively lean executive structure for a company processing sales engagement data

Geographic scope explicitly states 'No physical offices and related physical and availability specific controls are included since AltiSales does not have any physical servers and all data is processed on cloud services' - fully remote/cloud-native organization
The company name inconsistency between altisales.com (website) and altisales.io (security email) suggests possible domain migration or dual-domain setup
